</imports>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.58) — 30 imports target "analysis-engine"
- The zone name 'unit' is misleading — it conflates schema types, branch-work production code, and test helpers, which should be separated
- Coupling of 0.58 is near the warning threshold and reflects this zone's role as a schema hub depended on by many other zones
- The test helper file tests/unit/analyzers/zones-helpers.ts being grouped with production code indicates the zone boundary is driven by import edges rather than domain intent
- Coupling at 0.58 approaches the warning threshold (>0.6); monitor as new consumers of schema/v1.ts and data-files.ts are added.
- The zone mixes schema foundation files (v1.ts, data-files.ts), branch-work services, and a test helper — low cohesion (0.42) reflects this mixed responsibility.
- Extracting schema/v1.ts and schema/data-files.ts into a dedicated schema-foundation zone would reduce coupling and clarify the dependency graph for downstream consumers.
- Zone "branch-work-analysis" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Schema types v1.ts and data-files.ts residing inside branch-work-analysis act as a foundation layer consumed by 3+ zones, but inherit the coupling and mixed-content problems of their host zone — they have no zone-level isolation of their own
- The 30 outgoing imports from branch-work-analysis into analysis-engine combined with 7 return imports makes this pair the highest-traffic bidirectional coupling in the graph, accounting for 37 of the total cross-zone edges
- Schema foundation types (v1.ts, schema/index.ts) are co-located inside branch-work-analysis rather than a dedicated foundation zone; consumers importing schema types inadvertently couple to the branch-work-analysis zone boundary, making schema extraction riskier over time
- branch-work-analysis ↔ analysis-engine (30+7 crossings) is the dominant edge pair in the graph — 37 cross-zone imports between two zones signals that zone boundaries do not reflect real module seams; these zones likely share a common abstraction that should be extracted
- mcp.ts (a CLI command entry point) is co-located with domain analysis services inside branch-work-analysis — a CLI adapter living inside a domain zone violates the CLI-above-domain principle at the intra-zone level, independent of cross-zone import direction
- pr-markdown-template.ts is a presentation/templating concern embedded in a domain analysis zone — templates are output-layer artifacts and should live in cli-operations or a dedicated rendering layer
- workspace-aggregate.ts (aggregation service) and workspace.ts (CLI command) being in the same zone means domain aggregation logic and its CLI adapter share a zone boundary, making it impossible to evolve either independently without risking the other
- mcp.ts is a CLI command co-located with domain services in branch-work-analysis; CLI adapters must not share a zone boundary with domain logic they adapt — move mcp.ts to cli-operations to enforce CLI-above-domain layering within the zone, not just across zones
- pr-markdown-template.ts is a presentation-layer template embedded in a domain analysis zone; output templates have no domain responsibility and should reside in cli-operations or a dedicated output-rendering zone to prevent domain logic from acquiring a dependency on formatting details
- branch-work-analysis has BOTH below-midpoint cohesion (0.42) AND above-midpoint coupling (0.58) simultaneously — per the severity guide this combination defines a fragile zone; it is the only zone in this codebase that satisfies both fragility conditions at once
- The cross-zone import table uses the alias 'unit' for this zone while the canonical zone ID is 'branch-work-analysis' — the alias persists from a prior zone name and creates documentation ambiguity when tracing import edges across analysis passes
- workspace-aggregate.ts (domain service) and the CLI's workspace.ts share a 'workspace' naming prefix across two different zones with no enforced naming convention — the shared prefix implies a module family but the files are governed by different zone rules
- branch-work-analysis is the only zone with both cohesion below 0.5 and coupling above 0.5 — treat it as the highest-fragility zone in the codebase; prioritize extracting schema foundation types first (reduces coupling) and moving CLI artifacts out second (increases cohesion) before adding any new files to this zone
- The 'unit' alias used in cross-import tables for this zone is a residual name from a prior zone structure; update all documentation and tooling references to use the canonical ID 'branch-work-analysis' to prevent import tracing errors in future analysis passes
- workspace-aggregate.ts and workspace.ts (CLI) share a naming prefix implying a module family, but are governed by different zone boundaries with no enforced convention; document an explicit naming rule — domain aggregates use '-aggregate' suffix, CLI adapters use the bare noun — and verify all paired domain/CLI files follow it
- [call graph] 1363 internal calls, 272 outgoing, 579 incoming (cohesion: 0.83, coupling: 0.17)

</insights>
